case_name: YOGESHKUMAR BADHARSINH BARIA Versus DILIPKUMAR RANCHHODBHAI DAMOR & ANR.
court: High Court of Gujarat at Ahmedabad

compensation_breakdown.interest: 9% p.a. from the date of filing of the claim petition till its actual realization
compensation_breakdown.notes: Appeal enhanced Tribunal award from Rs.4,68,600/- to Rs.11,73,840/-. Court revised income, functional disability, future prospects, PSS and awarded future medical/prosthetic costs.

facts: The original claimant, employed in masonry/centering work and agricultural activities, suffered grievous injuries in a motor accident caused by rash and negligent driving of respondent no.1, resulting in amputation of his right leg below the knee. Medical records include an injury certificate dated 1 February 2017 and disability certificates indicating permanent disability (65%-70% for the limb; Tribunal accepted 35% for whole body). The claimant filed MACP No.695 of 2016 and the Tribunal awarded Rs.4,68,600/- on 8 February 2023. The appellant filed First Appeal under Section 173 MVA seeking enhancement of compensation.
arguments_claimant: Claimant argued the Tribunal undervalued monthly income and should have used minimum wages for a skilled worker (Rs.7,800/-) rather than Rs.4,000/-, and sought 40% addition for future prospects relying on Pranay Sethi. He argued functional disability from amputation must be considered for loss of future earnings, that pain, shock and suffering (PSS) should be higher, and that future medical/prosthetic expenses should be awarded.
arguments_respondent: Respondents did not appear for final hearing before the High Court. There is no record of a sustained challenge on negligence or liability (those issues attained finality); the contest at lower forum related to quantum of compensation.
ratio_decidendi: "The only question which falls for consideration of this Court in the present appeal is as to whether the Tribunal has committed any error in awarding amount of compensation in the facts of the case and the evidence brought on record?" The Court held that "the Tribunal ought to have considered the functional disability in the peculiar facts of the case once it has been established before the Tribunal that the original claimant has sustained amputation of right leg below knee." Relying on precedent, the Court reiterated that "The Tribunal should not mechanically apply the percentage of permanent disability as the percentage of economic loss or loss of earning capacity" and that assessment must consider the effect of permanent disability on earning capacity. Applying the legal fiction in the Note appended to the Second Schedule and Schedule I of the Employees' Compensation Act, the Court applied the Schedule entry for amputation below knee (treated as 50% loss of earning capacity) and accepted 40% towards future rise in income, using the multiplier method (multiplier 17) to compute future loss.
final_order: First appeal allowed; Tribunal award enhanced and claimant entitled to Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% interest from the date of filing of the claim petition till realization; respondents held jointly and severally liable. Enhanced amount to be deposited within eight weeks; Tribunal to disburse 50% to claimant and invest remaining 50% in a non-cumulative fixed deposit for five years (renewable).
summary: This is a High Court first appeal under Section 173 MVA by Yogeshkumar B. Baria challenging quantum of compensation after he suffered amputation of right leg below knee in a motor accident. The Court found the Tribunal erred by not adequately considering functional disability, future prospects and future medical/prosthetic expenses, and applied Schedule I (Employees' Compensation Act) and Supreme Court principles (Pranay Sethi, Rajkumar) to reassess income, disability and multiplier. The Tribunal award of Rs.4,68,600/- was enhanced to Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% interest and respondents ordered jointly and severally liable. Practical effect: higher award for amputee claimant including prosthetic costs and structured disbursement.
embedding_summary: Yogeshkumar Badharsinh Baria v. Dilipkumar Ranchhodbhai Damor, High Court of Gujarat, First Appeal C/FA/4321/2024, judgment dated 08/09/2025. Claimant (28 years at time of accident) suffered fracture and amputation of right leg below knee; injury certificate dated 1-2-2017 and disability certificates on record. Appeal under Section 173 Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 seeking enhancement of MACP No.695/2016 award (Tribunal had awarded Rs.4,68,600/-). Key legal issues: assessment of functional disability vs percentage permanent disability, application of Note to Second Schedule of MVA 1988 and Schedule I of Employees' Compensation Act 1923 (amputation below knee = 50%), application of Pranay Sethi (2017) for 40% future prospects, use of multiplier method (Sarla Verma) — multiplier 17 applied, and award of future medical/prosthetic expenses (per Sidram). Outcome: enhanced award Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% p.a. interest; respondents jointly and severally liable; deposit/disbursement directions (50% immediate, 50% in FD).
legal_principles: Do not mechanically equate percentage of permanent physical disability to percentage loss of earning capacity; assess functional impact on earning capacity

Apply Note to the Second Schedule of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 and Schedule I of the Employees' Compensation Act, 1923 to derive functional disability percentages (e.g., amputation below knee -> 50%)
Award future prospects (40%) in suitable cases following Pranay Sethi where victim is a living injured person with potential for income rise
Use multiplier-multiplicand method (Sarla Verma approach) to compute future loss of earnings; adopt appropriate multiplier (17 in this case)
Tribunals must award future medical expenses including prosthetic limb and replacement costs for amputees (per Sidram and related precedents)
Courts should enhance pain, shock and suffering awards where injuries and amputation warrant higher PSS
sections_cited: Note appended to the Second Schedule of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988
Second Schedule appended to the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988
Schedule I to the Employees' Compensation Act, 1923 (Workmen's Compensation Act, 1923)

bm25_text: YOGESHKUMAR BADHARSINH BARIA Versus DILIPKUMAR RANCHHODBHAI DAMOR & ANR. High Court of Gujarat at Ahmedabad 2025 The original claimant, employed in masonry/centering work and agricultural activities, suffered grievous injuries in a motor accident caused by rash and negligent driving of respondent no.1, resulting in amputation of his right leg below the knee. Medical records include an injury certificate dated 1 February 2017 and disability certificates indicating permanent disability (65%-70% for the limb; Tribunal accepted 35% for whole body). The claimant filed MACP No.695 of 2016 and the Tribunal awarded Rs.4,68,600/- on 8 February 2023. The appellant filed First Appeal under Section 173 MVA seeking enhancement of compensation. Claimant argued the Tribunal undervalued monthly income and should have used minimum wages for a skilled worker (Rs.7,800/-) rather than Rs.4,000/-, and sought 40% addition for future prospects relying on Pranay Sethi. He argued functional disability from amputation must be considered for loss of future earnings, that pain, shock and suffering (PSS) should be higher, and that future medical/prosthetic expenses should be awarded. Respondents did not appear for final hearing before the High Court. There is no record of a sustained challenge on negligence or liability (those issues attained finality); the contest at lower forum related to quantum of compensation. "The only question which falls for consideration of this Court in the present appeal is as to whether the Tribunal has committed any error in awarding amount of compensation in the facts of the case and the evidence brought on record?" The Court held that "the Tribunal ought to have considered the functional disability in the peculiar facts of the case once it has been established before the Tribunal that the original claimant has sustained amputation of right leg below knee." Relying on precedent, the Court reiterated that "The Tribunal should not mechanically apply the percentage of permanent disability as the percentage of economic loss or loss of earning capacity" and that assessment must consider the effect of permanent disability on earning capacity. Applying the legal fiction in the Note appended to the Second Schedule and Schedule I of the Employees' Compensation Act, the Court applied the Schedule entry for amputation below knee (treated as 50% loss of earning capacity) and accepted 40% towards future rise in income, using the multiplier method (multiplier 17) to compute future loss. First appeal allowed; Tribunal award enhanced and claimant entitled to Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% interest from the date of filing of the claim petition till realization; respondents held jointly and severally liable. Enhanced amount to be deposited within eight weeks; Tribunal to disburse 50% to claimant and invest remaining 50% in a non-cumulative fixed deposit for five years (renewable). This is a High Court first appeal under Section 173 MVA by Yogeshkumar B. Baria challenging quantum of compensation after he suffered amputation of right leg below knee in a motor accident. The Court found the Tribunal erred by not adequately considering functional disability, future prospects and future medical/prosthetic expenses, and applied Schedule I (Employees' Compensation Act) and Supreme Court principles (Pranay Sethi, Rajkumar) to reassess income, disability and multiplier. The Tribunal award of Rs.4,68,600/- was enhanced to Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% interest and respondents ordered jointly and severally liable. Practical effect: higher award for amputee claimant including prosthetic costs and structured disbursement. Yogeshkumar Badharsinh Baria v. Dilipkumar Ranchhodbhai Damor, High Court of Gujarat, First Appeal C/FA/4321/2024, judgment dated 08/09/2025. Claimant (28 years at time of accident) suffered fracture and amputation of right leg below knee; injury certificate dated 1-2-2017 and disability certificates on record. Appeal under Section 173 Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 seeking enhancement of MACP No.695/2016 award (Tribunal had awarded Rs.4,68,600/-). Key legal issues: assessment of functional disability vs percentage permanent disability, application of Note to Second Schedule of MVA 1988 and Schedule I of Employees' Compensation Act 1923 (amputation below knee = 50%), application of Pranay Sethi (2017) for 40% future prospects, use of multiplier method (Sarla Verma) — multiplier 17 applied, and award of future medical/prosthetic expenses (per Sidram). Outcome: enhanced award Rs.11,73,840/- with 9% p.a. interest; respondents jointly and severally liable; deposit/disbursement directions (50% immediate, 50% in FD). Court revised income, functional disability, future prospects, PSS and awarded future medical/prosthetic costs.
